title: Tetrastigma pisicarpum
intro: Tetrastigma pisicarpum, also known as the Chinese grapevine or wolfberry vine, is a type of vining plant native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. The health benefits of Tetrastigma pisicarpum include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Tetrastigma pisicarpum include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to support overall health and well-being, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine.
taxonomy: Tetrastigma pisicarpum belongs to the family Vitaceae. This plant is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. It is classified as a type of climbing vine or a woody climber. The Vitaceae family includes several species of grapes, figs, and other related plants. Tetrastigma pisicarpum is a species within this diverse and widespread plant family.
